note: Redirect exposes a content entity, a config settings object, two submodules, and a small service API. Cases span three difficulty tiers: `easy`/`recipe` (response-graded) probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; `medium`/`introspection` cases install a KNOWN redirect via `setup`, have the agent read it back from the live site (drush), grade on response text, then `cleanup`; `hard`/`execution` cases mutate live state and are graded by reset -> model -> verify against real redirect entities.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), create a permanent (301) URL redirect so that visits to the path `eval-old-page` are forwarded to the site's front page. Create the redirect as a real redirect entity on the live site; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A redirect entity with source path `eval-old-page`, status_code 301, and a non-empty target pointing to the front page (internal:/ , <front>, or route:<front>) exists on the live site. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), create a temporary (302) URL redirect so that visits to the path `eval-temp-page` are forwarded to `/node/1`. Create the redirect as a real redirect entity on the live site; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A redirect entity with source path `eval-temp-page`, status_code 302, and target node/1 (stored as internal:/node/1) exists on the live site. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), create a permanent (301) URL redirect so that visits to the path `eval-ext` are forwarded to the external URL https://www.drupal.org . Create the redirect as a real redirect entity on the live site; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A redirect entity with source path `eval-ext`, status_code 301, and a target that is an external http(s) URL (e.g. https://www.drupal.org) exists on the live site. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: A redirect already exists on this site for the path `eval-intro-src` — where does it point and with what HTTP status code? Inspect the live site (drush available) to answer.
evals.expected_output: The eval-intro-src redirect points to node/1 (stored internal:/node/1) with HTTP status code 302. Read back from live site state via drush.

evals.prompt: What status code (301 or 302) does the `eval-intro-src` redirect use on this site? Inspect the live site (drush available) to answer.
evals.expected_output: The eval-intro-src redirect uses HTTP status code 302 (temporary). Read back from live site state via drush.

evals.prompt: What is the target (destination) of the `eval-intro-src` redirect on this site? Inspect the live site (drush available) to answer.
evals.expected_output: The eval-intro-src redirect targets node/1 (stored as internal:/node/1). Read back from live site state via drush.

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, programmatically create a redirect from `old/path` to `node/1` with a 301 status code. Show the code.
evals.expected_output: Uses the Redirect entity: Redirect::create() then setSource('old/path'), setRedirect('node/1'), setStatusCode(301), save().

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to look up whether a given source path already has a redirect before creating a new one. Which service do I use and which method performs the main lookup?
evals.expected_output: The redirect.repository service (RedirectRepository); findMatchingRedirect() is the main lookup, findBySourcePath() also returns matches.

evals.prompt: I want Redirect to automatically create a redirect when a node's URL alias changes so SEO ranking survives. Which config setting controls this, and what config object holds it?
evals.expected_output: The auto_redirect key (default true) in the redirect.settings config object controls auto-creating a redirect when an alias changes.

evals.prompt: How do I change the site-wide default HTTP status code used for new redirects on a Drupal site? Give the config key and a drush command.
evals.expected_output: Set the default_status_code key (default 301) on redirect.settings, e.g. drush cset redirect.settings default_status_code 302.

evals.prompt: What are the two permission machine names Redirect provides, and which one gates the global settings form versus managing individual redirects?
evals.expected_output: 'administer redirects' gates creating/editing/deleting individual redirects; 'administer redirect settings' gates the global settings form.

evals.prompt: Which Redirect submodule logs 404s so I can turn frequent missing-page requests into redirects, and which submodule redirects between whole domains?
evals.expected_output: redirect_404 logs 404s and turns frequent misses into redirects; redirect_domain redirects between whole domains.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to alter the redirect response just before it is sent (e.g. add a message or change the target). Which hook do I implement?
Which Redirect setting controls whether the incoming query string is kept (passed through) when a request is redirected, and what is its default?
evals.expected_output: The passthrough_querystring key on redirect.settings (default true) keeps the incoming query string on redirect.
